title: Media list page
analysis:
Task requires building a media browser at /media (HTML page) and /api/media (JSON endpoint) with sortable, filterable, paginated table of all ingested media files, plus analysis badges.

Existing infrastructure:
- autopilot/db.py has CatalogDB with media_files CRUD (insert_media, get_media, list_all_media, list_by_status, update_media_status) and per-media has_* methods (has_transcript, has_detections, has_faces, has_embeddings, has_audio_events). No has_captions method exists yet.
- media_files schema: id TEXT PK, file_path TEXT, sha256_prefix TEXT, codec TEXT, resolution_w INT, resolution_h INT, fps REAL, duration_seconds REAL, created_at TEXT, gps_lat REAL, gps_lon REAL, audio_channels INT, status TEXT DEFAULT 'ingested', metadata_json TEXT.
- Related tables: transcripts, detections, faces, clip_embeddings, audio_events, captions — all keyed by media_id.
- autopilot/web/app.py: create_app(db_path) factory with Jinja2Templates, StaticFiles, currently only registers sse_router.
- base.html: dark-mode Tailwind CSS + HTMX template with nav including /media link.
- tests/test_web_app.py and tests/test_sse.py show patterns: app fixture using tmp_path for real DB, TestClient.
- No list_media_paginated or query method exists — need to add one to CatalogDB.

Approach:
1. Add has_captions() convenience method to CatalogDB (mirrors existing has_* pattern).
2. Add a query_media() method to CatalogDB that supports filtering, sorting, pagination, and returns analysis coverage flags via efficient LEFT JOIN subqueries (avoids N+1).
3. Create autopilot/web/routes/media.py with APIRouter containing GET /media (HTML) and GET /api/media (JSON).
4. Create templates: media/list.html (extends base.html) and partials/media_row.html (for HTMX partial swaps).
5. Register media_router in app.py alongside sse_router.
6. HTMX: filter changes swap the table body via hx-get targeting /api/media with Accept: text/html or a dedicated partial endpoint.

prerequisites.description: Create directory structure: autopilot/web/templates/media/ and autopilot/web/templates/partials/ directories

steps.description: Write test for CatalogDB.has_captions(media_id) in tests/test_media_list.py — mirrors existing has_transcript/has_detections pattern. Test returns False when no captions, True after inserting a caption for the media_id.
Add has_captions(media_id) method to CatalogDB in autopilot/db.py — SELECT 1 FROM captions WHERE media_id = ? LIMIT 1, returns bool. Follows exact pattern of has_transcript, has_detections, etc.

Write test for CatalogDB.query_media() method in tests/test_media_list.py. Tests: (1) test_query_media_returns_all_when_no_filters — insert 3 media, call query_media(), verify all 3 returned with total_count=3; (2) test_query_media_pagination — insert 5 media, call with per_page=2, page=2, verify 2 items returned and total_count=5; (3) test_query_media_text_filter — insert media with various filenames, filter with q='beach', verify only matching rows returned; (4) test_query_media_status_filter — insert media with different statuses, filter by status='analyzed'; (5) test_query_media_sort_by_filename — verify ascending/descending sort on filename; (6) test_query_media_sort_by_created_at — verify sort by created_at; (7) test_query_media_analysis_flags — insert media with/without transcript/detections/faces/embeddings/audio_events/captions, verify has_* flags are correct booleans in results.

Add query_media() method to CatalogDB that accepts q (text search on file_path), status, sort (column name), order (asc/desc), page, per_page. Uses a single SQL query with LEFT JOIN subqueries to compute analysis flags (has_transcript, has_detections, has_faces, has_embeddings, has_audio_events, has_captions) efficiently. Returns dict with 'items' list and 'total' count. Whitelist sort columns to prevent SQL injection.

Write tests for GET /api/media JSON endpoint in tests/test_media_list.py. Create app/client fixtures with pre-populated media data. Tests: (1) test_api_media_returns_json — GET /api/media returns 200 with JSON containing 'items' and 'total'; (2) test_api_media_pagination_params — verify ?page=2&per_page=2 returns correct subset; (3) test_api_media_filter_by_status — verify ?status=analyzed filters correctly; (4) test_api_media_text_search — verify ?q=beach filters by filename; (5) test_api_media_sort — verify ?sort=filename&order=asc returns sorted results; (6) test_api_media_items_have_analysis_flags — verify each item has has_transcript, has_detections, etc. boolean fields.

Create autopilot/web/routes/media.py with APIRouter. Implement GET /api/media endpoint that accepts query params (q, status, page, per_page, sort, order), creates CatalogDB from request.app.state.db_path, calls query_media(), returns JSON response. Update routes/__init__.py to export media_router. Register in app.py.

Write tests for GET /media HTML page in tests/test_media_list.py. Tests: (1) test_media_page_returns_html — GET /media returns 200 with text/html content-type; (2) test_media_page_extends_base — verify response contains nav elements from base.html (e.g., 'Autopilot Video'); (3) test_media_page_contains_table — verify response contains a table element with expected column headers (Filename, Duration, Resolution, etc.); (4) test_media_page_contains_filter_controls — verify filter inputs exist (text search, status dropdown); (5) test_media_page_has_htmx_attributes — verify hx-get and hx-target attributes exist on filter elements for partial swap.

Create autopilot/web/templates/media/list.html extending base.html. Include: page title 'Media', sortable table with columns (Thumbnail placeholder, Filename, Duration, Resolution, Created, Location, Status, Analysis badges), filter controls (text search input, status dropdown, date range inputs), pagination controls. Add hx-get='/api/media' on filter form targeting table body. Create autopilot/web/templates/partials/media_row.html for a single table row. Implement GET /media route in media.py that renders list.html with initial data.

Write tests for HTMX partial response in tests/test_media_list.py. Tests: (1) test_api_media_htmx_returns_partial — GET /api/media with HX-Request:true header returns HTML table rows (partial) not full page; (2) test_partial_media_row_has_analysis_badges — verify partial response contains analysis badge icons/indicators for media with analysis data; (3) test_partial_media_row_duration_format — verify duration is formatted as HH:MM:SS; (4) test_partial_media_row_resolution_display — verify resolution displayed as WxH.

Implement HTMX partial support in media.py: when HX-Request header is present on GET /api/media, render partials/media_row.html for each item and return HTML fragment instead of JSON. Add duration formatting helper (seconds -> HH:MM:SS) and resolution formatting to the template context. Ensure analysis badges render as icons/indicators based on has_* flags. Verify all tests pass.

Write integration tests in tests/test_media_list.py. Tests: (1) test_media_list_empty_database — GET /media with no media files shows empty state; (2) test_media_list_pagination_navigation — insert 60 media files, verify first page shows 50, pagination indicates 2 pages; (3) test_media_list_combined_filters — filter by status AND text search simultaneously; (4) test_query_media_date_range_filter — filter by date range (date_from, date_to).

Add date range filtering support to query_media (date_from, date_to params filtering on created_at). Add empty state display to list.html template. Ensure pagination navigation is correct with page count. Final cleanup and verification that all tests pass.

design_decisions.decision: Add query_media() method to CatalogDB rather than building SQL in the route handler
design_decisions.rationale: Keeps database logic in the DB layer consistent with existing patterns (list_all_media, list_by_status, list_jobs). The route handler stays thin, just mapping query params to method args. Also makes the query testable independently of FastAPI.

design_decisions.decision: Use LEFT JOIN subqueries for analysis flags instead of N+1 has_* calls
design_decisions.rationale: The task explicitly requires efficient SQL. Using EXISTS subqueries in a single query (SELECT m.*, EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM transcripts WHERE media_id = m.id) as has_transcript, ...) avoids N+1 queries when listing 50 items per page.

design_decisions.decision: Dual response mode for /api/media: JSON by default, HTML partial when HX-Request header present
design_decisions.rationale: Keeps a single endpoint for both initial page load (JSON used by template) and HTMX partial swaps (returns rendered table rows). The HX-Request header is set automatically by HTMX. This avoids creating a separate /api/media/partial endpoint.

design_decisions.decision: Whitelist sort columns to prevent SQL injection
design_decisions.rationale: Since sort column names are interpolated into SQL (can't use parameterized queries for column names), we validate against an explicit whitelist: {filename, duration_seconds, created_at, resolution_w, status}.

design_decisions.decision: Use file_path for filename display and search rather than adding a separate filename column
design_decisions.rationale: media_files schema has file_path but no filename column. We'll extract the filename portion in SQL (or Python) for display, and text search will use LIKE on file_path. This avoids schema changes.

design_decisions.decision: Render analysis badges as simple CSS-styled spans with title attributes rather than SVG icons
design_decisions.rationale: The task says placeholder icons. Using styled text badges (e.g., 'ASR', 'YOLO', 'Face', 'Emb', 'Audio', 'Cap') with color coding is simpler to implement and test than SVG icons, while still conveying the information clearly.

design_decisions.decision: Create tests in a single new file tests/test_media_list.py rather than adding to test_web_app.py
design_decisions.rationale: test_web_app.py covers the app skeleton and base template. Media list is a substantial feature with its own DB methods, endpoints, and templates — a dedicated test file keeps things organized and avoids merge conflicts.

reuse.what: CatalogDB has_* pattern (has_transcript, has_detections, has_faces, has_embeddings, has_audio_events)
reuse.how: Follow exact same pattern for has_captions. Also reference these as the canonical analysis flags to expose in query_media results.
Used in tests to seed the database with mock media files with varying attributes (status, resolution, duration, GPS, created_at).

reuse.what: CatalogDB upsert methods (upsert_transcript, upsert_caption, batch_insert_detections, batch_insert_faces, batch_insert_embeddings, batch_insert_audio_events)
reuse.how: Used in tests to seed analysis data so that analysis badges can be verified.
Reuse exact same _get_db helper pattern for media routes to create CatalogDB from app state.
Follow same pattern: app fixture creates FastAPI via create_app(tmp_path), client fixture wraps with TestClient.
Follow same pattern: create CatalogDB with real file path (not :memory:) so the app can open its own connection, use autocommit for test convenience.
media/list.html extends base.html, fills {% block title %}, {% block content %}, and {% block scripts %} blocks.
Use same dark:bg-surface-* and dark:text-* classes for consistent dark-mode styling in media templates.
Use for pure CatalogDB unit tests (has_captions, query_media) that don't need FastAPI.
